rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in component implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings and assignments.
Use assign statements only for existing output features or declared auxiliary variables in implementation scope.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting annex blocks at the correct component or implementation scope.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scope; use type scope for dependency guarantees.
Do not include implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Implementation contracts use assign for binding outputs to values or auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; keep them in type scope for deterministic dependencies.
Do not use range comparisons or 0/1 equality on Boolean or opaque data ports unless numeric scalar representation is confirmed.
irrelevant_topics: assume/guarantee syntax and validator-driven repair patterns
AADL structural extraction and component properties
